description: AI sprint runner — executes prd.json backlogs via AI agents with autonomous failure recovery
quality_checks: uv run ruff check ralph.py tests/
uv run ruff format --check ralph.py tests/

epic_addenda.M1: All implementation code goes in ralph.py only — no sub-modules or new Python files. No shell=True anywhere. TaskTracker is the sole reader/writer of prd.json. All public functions must have complete type hints. Use named constants from the Constants block, never inline magic numbers. Do NOT touch prd.json or progress.txt — the orchestrator manages those.

tasks.title: scaffold_constants_exceptions_dataclasses
tasks.description: Establish the foundational building blocks of ralph.py: the module docstring and shebang, the full Constants block, all typed exception classes, and all dataclasses. This is the base layer every other class imports from. No logic — just declarations. See DESIGN.md sections: Constants, Exception Hierarchy, Data Classes.
acceptance_criteria: ralph.py has a shebang (#!/usr/bin/env python3) and module docstring explaining the tool's purpose

Constants block defines all values from DESIGN.md including DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS, CI_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS, all timeout constants, MAIN_BRANCH, LOG_FILE_NAME, PRD_FILE, PROGRESS_FILE, DEFAULT_OPENCODE_MODEL, GEMINI_MODEL

Exception hierarchy: RalphError base class plus BranchSyncError, BranchExistsError, RemoteNotSSHError, CITimeoutError, CIFailedFatal, PRDGuardViolation, CoderFailedError, ReviewerFailedError, PreflightError, PlanInvalidError, AgentSandboxViolation — all subclass RalphError
Dataclasses defined: Config, PRInfo, CIResult, ReviewResult, PreCommitResult, BranchStatus, TaskResult, PlanCheckResult, TestQualityResult, ReviewQualityResult — with all fields from DESIGN.md
Config dataclass includes tdd_mode: bool and max_test_write_rounds: int
uv sync --extra dev completes without error
uv run python ralph.py exits 0
Tests verify each exception is a subclass of RalphError and can be raised and caught
Tests verify each dataclass can be instantiated with its required fields

tasks.title: ralph_logger
tasks.description: Implement RalphLogger: dual-stream logger that writes to stdout and a log file simultaneously. Fixed-width level prefix. No Python logging module — direct print + file append. See DESIGN.md: RalphLogger.
acceptance_criteria: RalphLogger(log_path: Path) constructor opens or creates the log file
Methods: info(), warn(), error(), fatal() each accepting a message string
Each log line format: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message' where LEVEL is padded to 5 chars (INFO , WARN , ERROR, FATAL)
Every call writes to both stdout and the log file
fatal() writes the message then raises SystemExit(1)
Tests use tmp_path; verify log file is created, correct format, dual-stream output, fatal() raises SystemExit

tasks.title: subprocess_runner
tasks.description: Implement SubprocessRunner: the single wrapper around subprocess.run() used by every component. Key constraint: never shell=True. Supports env var removal for nested-Claude mitigation. See DESIGN.md: SubprocessRunner.
acceptance_criteria: SubprocessRunner(logger: RalphLogger) constructor
run(cmd: list[str], env_removals: list[str] = [], timeout: int = SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT_SECS, cwd: Path | None = None, check: bool = False) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess
Never uses shell=True under any circumstance
env_removals strips named keys from the child process environment without modifying the parent process os.environ
Logs the command (joined) at INFO level before running
Raises subprocess.CalledProcessError on non-zero exit when check=True
Tests verify: env var is absent in child when listed in env_removals, parent os.environ is unchanged, command is logged, CalledProcessError raised on failure with check=True

tasks.title: task_tracker
tasks.description: Implement TaskTracker: the sole owner of prd.json and progress.txt. No other class reads or writes these files. Every write method does a fresh json.load() — never uses cached state. See DESIGN.md: TaskTracker.
acceptance_criteria: TaskTracker(prd_path: Path, progress_path: Path, runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor
load() -> dict reads and returns prd.json from disk each call
get_next_task() -> dict | None returns the first task where completed=false, owner != 'human', decomposed != true, and all depends_on task IDs are completed; returns None if none found
get_task_by_id(task_id: str) -> dict | None
count_remaining() -> int counts incomplete ralph-owned non-decomposed tasks
get_quality_checks() -> list[str] returns quality_checks from prd.json
mark_complete(task_id: str) does fresh load, sets completed=true, writes back
append_progress(task_id, title, pr_number, today) appends a formatted line to progress.txt
commit_tracking(task_id, title) runs: git add prd.json progress.txt, git commit, git push origin main via SubprocessRunner
add_task(task: dict) inserts task at end of tasks list, writes back
mark_decomposed(task_id: str) sets decomposed=true on matching task, writes back
Tests use tmp_path for prd.json and progress.txt; verify fresh-load behaviour, depends_on filtering, human task skipping, decomposed task skipping

tasks.title: plan_checker_structural
tasks.description: Implement PlanChecker with Tier 1 structural validation, complexity inference, and auto-decomposition of complexity-3 tasks. The AI tier (--validate-plan) is M2. See DESIGN.md: PlanChecker.
acceptance_criteria: PlanChecker(task_tracker: TaskTracker, ai_runner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor

check_structural(prd: dict) -> list[str] validates: required fields present on each incomplete task (id, title, description, acceptance_criteria, owner, completed), acceptance_criteria is non-empty list, depends_on task IDs exist and are completed

_infer_complexity(task: dict) -> int scores 1-3: AC count >4 adds 1, description word count >80 adds 1, files count >3 adds 1, keywords 'refactor'/'migrate'/'redesign' in description add 1; clamped to 1-3

auto_decompose(prd: dict) -> int decomposes tasks where complexity=3 and no 'parent' field; calls AIRunner with decompose_prompt; creates subtasks with IDs like TASK-01a/b/c via TaskTracker.add_task(); marks parent with TaskTracker.mark_decomposed(); returns count
run(prd: dict, ai_check: bool = False) -> PlanCheckResult combines structural check and auto_decompose
Raises PlanInvalidError with all error messages if structural errors found
Tests: valid plan passes, missing required field caught, empty ACs caught, unresolved depends_on caught, complexity inference correct for boundary cases

tasks.title: branch_manager
tasks.description: Implement BranchManager: all git operations. Critical: SSH-only enforcement, reset --hard sync (never pull --ff-only). See DESIGN.md: BranchManager and lessons #1, #6.
acceptance_criteria: BranchManager(repo_dir: Path, runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor
verify_ssh_remote() raises RemoteNotSSHError if 'git remote get-url origin' output does not start with 'git@'
ensure_main_up_to_date() runs: git checkout main, git fetch origin main, git reset --hard origin/main — in that order; never uses pull --ff-only

checkout_or_create(branch: str, resume: bool) -> BranchStatus: if branch exists and resume=False raises BranchExistsError; if branch exists and resume=True checks it out; otherwise creates it from main
push_branch(branch: str) calls verify_ssh_remote() before pushing
delete_local(branch: str, ignore_missing: bool = False) deletes local branch; silently skips if ignore_missing=True and branch not found
delete_remote(branch: str, ignore_missing: bool = False) deletes remote branch
merge_and_cleanup(branch: str) checks out main, runs ensure_main_up_to_date(), deletes local branch
sanitise_branch_name(title: str) -> str replaces non-alphanumeric with hyphens, lowercases, truncates to 40 chars
Tests mock SubprocessRunner and verify exact git commands issued; verify SSH check fires before push

tasks.title: pr_manager
tasks.description: Implement PRManager: all gh pr operations. Parses PR numbers with regex, not grep. Handles race condition on fresh PRs with retry. See DESIGN.md: PRManager.
acceptance_criteria: PRManager(runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor
create(branch: str, title: str, body: str) -> PRInfo: runs gh pr create, parses PR number with re.search(r'(\d+)$', url), returns PRInfo
get_existing(branch: str) -> PRInfo | None: returns open PR for branch if one exists
get_diff(pr_number: int, retries: int = 5, delay: int = 10) -> str: retries with delay if diff is empty (fresh PR race condition)
get_diff_for_file(pr_number: int, filepath: str) -> str
get_checks(pr_number: int) -> list[dict]: returns parsed JSON from 'gh pr checks --json name,state,conclusion,required'
merge(pr_number: int) runs squash merge via gh pr merge --squash --auto
close(pr_number: int, reason: str) posts reason as PR comment then closes
Tests mock SubprocessRunner; verify retry logic on empty diff; verify PR number regex parsing

tasks.title: prd_guard
tasks.description: Implement PRDGuard: pre-merge safety check that aborts if the coder touched prd.json. Threshold is 0 — any modification is a violation. See DESIGN.md: PRDGuard.
acceptance_criteria: PRDGuard(pr_manager: PRManager, logger: RalphLogger) constructor

check(pr_number: int) -> None: gets prd.json diff via PRManager.get_diff_for_file(); raises PRDGuardViolation if any line matches r'^\+.*"completed":\s*true' or if any '+' lines exist in the prd.json diff at all
PRDGuardViolation message includes the PR number and offending diff lines
Tests: clean diff passes; diff with completed:true raises PRDGuardViolation; any added line in prd.json raises PRDGuardViolation

tasks.title: prompt_builder
tasks.description: Implement PromptBuilder: all prompt templates as pure static methods. No logic here — stateless text assembly only. Includes epic_addenda injection, TDD prompts, and decomposition prompt. See DESIGN.md: PromptBuilder.
acceptance_criteria: PromptBuilder has no __init__ and no instance state — all methods are @staticmethod

coder_prompt(task: dict, coder: str, prd: dict, resume: bool = False) -> str: includes task description, all ACs numbered, files list, epic_addenda if task epic matches prd epic_addenda, explicit instruction not to touch prd.json/progress.txt, and resume note if resume=True
precommit_fix_prompt(task: dict, precommit_output: str) -> str
test_fix_prompt(task: dict, test_output: str) -> str

reviewer_prompt(task: dict, diff: str, prd: dict, round_num: int) -> str: includes all 6 review categories, epic_addenda if present, instruction to output APPROVED or CHANGES REQUESTED with file:line references
review_fix_prompt(task: dict, review_text: str) -> str
ci_fix_prompt(task: dict, failure_log: str) -> str
pr_body(task: dict) -> str
plan_check_prompt(tasks: list[dict]) -> str: asks AI to flag [WARN] untestable ACs, non-atomic tasks, contradictions
test_writer_prompt(task: dict) -> str: instructs agent to write FAILING tests only, not to implement the module under test, tests must fail with ImportError or AssertionError
test_quality_prompt(task: dict, test_source: str, ast_report: str) -> str: asks AI to flag [HOLLOW] tests that don't genuinely verify their AC
decompose_prompt(task: dict) -> str: asks AI to break task into 2-4 atomic subtasks as JSON list
Tests verify each prompt contains required keywords/sections; reviewer_prompt includes addendum when epic_addenda key matches task epic

tasks.title: ai_runner
tasks.description: Implement AIRunner: subprocess wrappers for all AI backends (claude, gemini, opencode). Complexity-based model routing. Nested-Claude session detection. Separate test-writer role. See DESIGN.md: AIRunner and lesson #8.
acceptance_criteria: AIRunner(runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
_is_nested_claude_session() -> bool returns True if 'CLAUDECODE' in os.environ

assign_agents(task: dict) -> tuple[str, str]: returns (coder, reviewer) based on config.model_mode and task complexity; complexity 1 → kimi/gemini-flash, 2 → gemini-pro/claude-sonnet, 3 → claude-opus/gemini-ultra; respects --claude-only/--gemini-only/--opencode-only overrides
run_coder(agent: str, prompt: str, cwd: Path) -> bool: invokes the agent subprocess, returns True on success
run_reviewer(agent: str, prompt: str) -> str: returns reviewer output; if agent=='claude' and _is_nested_claude_session(), logs warning and falls back to gemini
run_test_writer(prompt: str, cwd: Path) -> bool: always uses a different model from the coder for the same task (enforced by assign_agents)
All Claude calls pass env_removals=['CLAUDECODE'] to SubprocessRunner
Tests mock SubprocessRunner; verify nested-Claude fallback triggers; verify complexity routing for each tier; verify CLAUDECODE is removed from env on Claude calls

tasks.title: precommit_gate
tasks.description: Implement PreCommitGate: runs pre-commit hooks, invokes coder fix loop on failure, warns after max rounds. See DESIGN.md: PreCommitGate.
acceptance_criteria: PreCommitGate(runner: SubprocessRunner, ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
run(task: dict, prd: dict, branch_dir: Path) -> PreCommitResult
Runs 'uv run pre-commit run --all-files' via SubprocessRunner with cwd=branch_dir
Detects failure by exit code only — not by grepping output
On failure: invokes AIRunner.run_coder() with PromptBuilder.precommit_fix_prompt(), increments round counter
Retries up to config.max_precommit_rounds
After max rounds still failing: logs warning, returns PreCommitResult(passed=False, rounds_used=max_rounds)
On success: returns PreCommitResult(passed=True, rounds_used=N)
Tests mock SubprocessRunner and AIRunner; verify retry count; verify exit-code-based detection

tasks.title: test_runner
tasks.description: Implement TestRunner: runs quality_checks from prd.json, invokes coder fix loop on failure. See DESIGN.md: TestRunner.
acceptance_criteria: TestRunner(runner: SubprocessRunner, ai_runner: AIRunner, task_tracker: TaskTracker, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
run(task: dict, prd: dict) -> TestResult
Reads quality_checks via TaskTracker.get_quality_checks()
Runs each command via SubprocessRunner; failure = non-zero exit code
On any failure: invokes AIRunner.run_coder() with PromptBuilder.test_fix_prompt(), retries all checks
Retries up to config.max_test_fix_rounds
After max rounds still failing: logs warning, returns TestResult(passed=False, rounds_used=N)
Tests mock SubprocessRunner, AIRunner, TaskTracker; verify all checks run; verify fix loop fires on failure

tasks.title: test_writer
tasks.description: Implement TestWriter: TDD mode component that invokes a separate AI agent to write failing tests before the coder starts. The test writer must be a different model from the eventual coder. See DESIGN.md: TestWriter.
acceptance_criteria: TestWriter(ai_runner: AIRunner, runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor
write_tests(task: dict, branch_dir: Path) -> Path: invokes AIRunner.run_test_writer() with PromptBuilder.test_writer_prompt(task)
Discovers the test file written by the agent (looks in tests/ directory for files matching test_{task_title}*.py)
Commits the test file to the current branch via SubprocessRunner (git add, git commit)
Returns the Path to the committed test file
The model used for test writing is always different from the model that will be used for coding the same task (enforced via AIRunner.assign_agents returning distinct test_writer model)
Tests mock AIRunner and SubprocessRunner; verify commit is issued; verify path returned

tasks.title: test_quality_checker
tasks.description: Implement TestQualityChecker: validates tests written by TestWriter are not hollow. Two-tier: AST analysis first, then AI semantic review. See DESIGN.md: TestQualityChecker.
acceptance_criteria: TestQualityChecker(ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor

_ast_checks(test_source: str, task: dict) -> list[str]: uses ast module to detect: test functions with no assertions, assert True/assert <constant> patterns, pass-only test bodies, test count < len(acceptance_criteria), no import of module under test
check(task: dict, test_file_path: Path) -> TestQualityResult: runs _ast_checks; if issues found returns failed result immediately without calling AI
When _ast_checks passes: invokes AIRunner with PromptBuilder.test_quality_prompt(), parses output for '[HOLLOW] <test_name>: <reason>' lines
Returns TestQualityResult with hollow_tests, deterministic_issues, ai_issues populated
run(task: dict, test_file_path: Path, test_writer: TestWriter, rounds: int = 0) -> TestQualityResult: retries TestWriter up to config.max_test_write_rounds if quality fails
Tests: file with assert True fails T1; file with no assertions fails T1; valid test file passes T1; T2 only called when T1 passes

tasks.title: review_loop
tasks.description: Implement ReviewLoop: drives the reviewer agent, parses verdict, invokes coder fix loop on CHANGES REQUESTED, and passes every review through ReviewQualityChecker. See DESIGN.md: ReviewLoop.
acceptance_criteria: ReviewLoop(pr_manager: PRManager, ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
run(task: dict, pr_number: int, prd: dict, coder: str, reviewer: str) -> ReviewResult
Gets PR diff via PRManager.get_diff(); sends to reviewer via AIRunner.run_reviewer()
Passes each review through ReviewQualityChecker before acting on it; on quality failure retries with different reviewer
Parses verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED takes precedence if both strings appear in output
If unclear verdict: treats as APPROVED and logs warning
On CHANGES_REQUESTED: invokes coder with PromptBuilder.review_fix_prompt(), pushes branch, re-reviews
After config.max_review_rounds: logs warning, returns ReviewResult(verdict='CHANGES_REQUESTED_MAX_REACHED', rounds_used=N)
On APPROVED: returns ReviewResult(verdict='APPROVED', rounds_used=N)
Tests mock PRManager, AIRunner, ReviewQualityChecker; verify CHANGES REQUESTED precedence logic; verify fix loop

tasks.title: review_quality_checker
tasks.description: Implement ReviewQualityChecker: validates every review is substantive before ReviewLoop acts on it. Deterministic tier first, AI meta-review on --deep-review-check. See DESIGN.md: ReviewQualityChecker.
acceptance_criteria: ReviewQualityChecker(ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
check(review_text: str, previous_reviews: list[str]) -> ReviewQualityResult

Tier 1 deterministic checks (all must pass): word count >= 80, contains APPROVE or REQUEST CHANGES (case-insensitive), contains at least one file path or line reference matching r'\w+\.py:\d+|\w+/\w+\.\w+', is not identical to the last entry in previous_reviews
Returns ReviewQualityResult(acceptable=False, reason=<specific failure>) on any Tier 1 failure
Tier 2 AI check: only runs when Tier 1 passes AND config has deep_review_check=True; sends review + task ACs to AIRunner; parses for PASS/FAIL
On quality failure: ReviewLoop retries with different reviewer agent; after 2 failures from different agents sets config.skip_review=True for this task and logs REVIEWER_QUALITY_FAILED
Tests: review < 80 words fails; review without verdict fails; review without file refs fails; identical to previous fails; valid review passes T1

tasks.title: ci_poller_parent
tasks.description: Parent task — decomposed into M1-17a and M1-17b.

tasks.title: ci_poller_polling_and_run_id_pinning
tasks.description: Implement CIPoller's polling layer: waiting for CI completion using run-ID pinning to avoid the stale-data race condition where gh pr checks returns results from the previous run after a fix push. See DESIGN.md: CIPoller and lesson #6.
acceptance_criteria: CIPoller(runner: SubprocessRunner, ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
_get_latest_run_id(branch: str) -> str: runs 'gh run list --branch <branch> --json databaseId --jq .[0].databaseId'
_wait_for_run(run_id: str) -> str: polls 'gh run view <run_id> --json status,conclusion' every CI_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS; returns 'PASSED' or 'FAILED'; raises CITimeoutError after CI_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS
_wait_for_new_run(branch: str, prev_run_id: str, timeout: int = 180) -> str: polls gh run list every 10s until a different run ID appears; raises CITimeoutError if no new run after timeout
wait_for_completion(pr_number: int, branch: str) -> CIResult: calls _get_latest_run_id then _wait_for_run; treats empty checks list as PENDING
Uses 'conclusion' field, not 'state' field, for pass/fail determination
Tests mock SubprocessRunner; verify run-ID pinning logic; verify CITimeoutError raised correctly

tasks.title: ci_poller_fix_loop
tasks.description: Implement CIPoller's fix loop: on CI failure, fetches logs, invokes coder, pushes fix, waits for new run ID before re-polling. Handles required-vs-optional check filtering. See DESIGN.md: CIPoller.
acceptance_criteria: wait_and_fix(task: dict, pr_number: int, branch: str, prd: dict) -> CIResult builds on wait_for_completion
On FAILED: fetches last 150 lines of 'gh run view --log-failed' output
Invokes AIRunner.run_coder() with PromptBuilder.ci_fix_prompt(task, failure_log)
Pushes fix via SubprocessRunner, then calls _wait_for_new_run() before re-polling — never polls immediately after push
Filters CI checks to only block on required=true checks (optional failing checks log warning only)
After config.max_ci_fix_rounds still failing: raises CIFailedFatal
Returns CIResult(passed=True, rounds_used=N) on success
Tests mock SubprocessRunner and AIRunner; verify _wait_for_new_run called after push; verify optional checks don't block; verify CIFailedFatal raised after max rounds

tasks.title: orchestrator_parent
tasks.description: Parent task — decomposed into M1-18a, M1-18b, M1-18c.

tasks.title: orchestrator_preflight_and_task_selection
tasks.description: Implement Orchestrator's preflight validation and task selection logic. The Orchestrator composes all components. See DESIGN.md: Orchestrator and lesson #9.
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator(config: Config, logger: RalphLogger) constructor instantiates all component classes

_preflight() validates in order: gh CLI available, git CLI available, SSH remote (via BranchManager.verify_ssh_remote()), prd.json not dirty vs origin ('git diff --quiet origin/main -- prd.json'), PlanChecker.run() passes structural checks
_preflight() logs prominent WARNING if CLAUDECODE env var is set: 'Running inside Claude Code session — reviewer unavailable, falling back to --skip-review'
_preflight() raises PreflightError with clear message on any failure
_check_stop_conditions(task: dict | None) -> str | None: returns stop reason string if sprint should end (no tasks, human task next), else None
Tests mock all components; verify preflight fires in correct order; verify dirty prd.json is caught; verify CLAUDECODE warning logs

tasks.title: orchestrator_standard_mode_state_machine
tasks.description: Implement Orchestrator._run_task() for standard mode: the full per-task state machine from ensure_main_up_to_date through mark_complete. Each step logs entry and exit. See DESIGN.md: Per-Task State Machine (standard mode).
acceptance_criteria: _run_task(task: dict) -> TaskResult implements the standard mode state machine in exact order from DESIGN.md
BranchSyncError → TaskResult(fatal=True)
BranchExistsError (no resume) → TaskResult(fatal=True)
CoderFailedError → TaskResult(fatal=True)
PreCommitGate failure after max rounds → logs WARN, continues
TestRunner failure after max rounds → logs WARN, continues
CalledProcessError on push → TaskResult(fatal=True)
ReviewLoop max rounds exceeded → logs WARN, continues to CI
CIFailedFatal / CITimeoutError → TaskResult(fatal=True)
PRDGuardViolation → closes PR, TaskResult(fatal=True)
On success: calls TaskTracker.mark_complete(), append_progress(), commit_tracking() — in that order
Returns TaskResult(fatal=False) on success
Integration test mocks all components and verifies correct execution order via call sequence tracking

tasks.title: orchestrator_tdd_mode_and_main_loop
tasks.description: Implement TDD mode branch in _run_task() and the main run() loop. TDD mode inserts TestWriter + TestQualityChecker before the coder. See DESIGN.md: Per-Task State Machine (TDD mode).
acceptance_criteria: When config.tdd_mode=True, _run_task() runs TestWriter.write_tests() then TestQualityChecker.run() before AIRunner.run_coder()
If TestQualityChecker fails after max_test_write_rounds: returns TaskResult(fatal=True) with reason
Coder is invoked only after TestQualityChecker confirms tests are not hollow
run(max_iterations: int) -> None: main loop; calls _preflight() once, then iterates: get_next_task, _check_stop_conditions, _run_task; stops on fatal TaskResult or stop condition
Logs iteration number and task ID at start of each iteration
Logs clean exit reason when loop ends normally
Tests: TDD mode verifies TestWriter called before run_coder; standard mode verifies TestWriter NOT called; main loop stops on fatal task result

tasks.title: cli_entry_point
tasks.description: Implement the click CLI entry point with all flags, the rzilla command (via pyproject.toml), and --dry-run mode. This is the last task — all other classes must exist first. See DESIGN.md: CLI Interface.
acceptance_criteria: Click command named 'main' decorated with all flags: --max, --skip-review, --tdd, --claude-only, --gemini-only, --opencode-only, --opencode-model, --resume, --max-test-fix-rounds, --max-test-write-rounds, --task, --validate-plan, --no-decompose, --deep-review-check, --dry-run, --repo-dir
Flags map to corresponding Config dataclass fields
main() constructs Config, RalphLogger, Orchestrator and calls orchestrator.run()
--dry-run prints each pending task title and estimated action without invoking any AI calls or git operations, then exits 0
Shebang (#!/usr/bin/env python3) at top of ralph.py makes ./ralph.py executable
'rzilla' entry point in pyproject.toml resolves to ralph:main
uv run rzilla --help exits 0 and lists all flags
uv run rzilla --dry-run exits 0 against the project's own prd.json
Tests: --help exits 0; unknown flag exits non-zero; --dry-run against fixture prd.json exits 0

tasks.title: ralph_init_discovery_wizard
tasks.description: Implement the 'ralph init' CLI command and DiscoveryWizard class. DiscoveryWizard asks 6 interactive questions (product description, language/runtime/package-manager, test framework + coverage tool, quality gate commands, human-only steps, out-of-scope items) and produces a ProjectSpec dataclass. 'ralph init' uses ProjectSpec to write: prd.json scaffold (with epic_addenda and quality_checks populated), CODER_INSTRUCTIONS.md, REVIEWER_INSTRUCTIONS.md, and a git hook blocking direct commits to main. No AI calls — pure interactive I/O. See DESIGN.md: DiscoveryWizard.
acceptance_criteria: DiscoveryWizard(io: IO) class with a run() -> ProjectSpec method that asks exactly 6 questions in order
ProjectSpec dataclass with fields: description, language, runtime, package_manager, test_framework, coverage_tool, quality_checks (list[str]), human_steps (list[str]), out_of_scope (list[str])

'ralph init' click command invokes DiscoveryWizard, writes prd.json scaffold with epic_addenda and quality_checks from ProjectSpec, writes CODER_INSTRUCTIONS.md and REVIEWER_INSTRUCTIONS.md to the repo root
Git hook written to .git/hooks/pre-push that blocks direct pushes to main with a clear error message
prd.json scaffold includes: project name (from description), quality_checks from ProjectSpec answers, empty tasks list, epic_addenda stub
Human-only steps from question 5 become owner: 'human' placeholder tasks in the generated prd.json
Tests use monkeypatch/mock for stdin; verify ProjectSpec fields match answers; verify prd.json and instruction files are created; verify git hook file exists and is executable

tasks.title: prd_validator
tasks.description: Implement PrdValidator: shared validation layer used by both PrdGenerator (M3-03) and PlanChecker. Enforces 4 rules: description >= 100 chars; at least one AC references a file path (contains '.py', 'tests/', or a filename pattern); no credential strings in ralph-owned task descriptions (regex: password|secret|token|api.?key, case-insensitive); all depends_on IDs exist in the tasks list. Raises PlanInvalidError with field name and reason on any failure. See DESIGN.md: PrdValidator.
acceptance_criteria: PrdValidator class with validate(task: dict, all_task_ids: set[str]) -> None method
Rule 1: raises PlanInvalidError if task description is shorter than 100 characters
Rule 2: raises PlanInvalidError if no acceptance criterion contains a file path pattern (r'[\w/]+\.py|tests/')
Rule 3: raises PlanInvalidError if description matches r'(?i)(password|secret|api.?key|token)' for ralph-owned tasks
Rule 4: raises PlanInvalidError if any depends_on ID is not in all_task_ids
PlanChecker.check_structural() calls PrdValidator.validate() for each incomplete task instead of duplicating the logic

Tests: each rule has a dedicated test — short description fails R1, description without file ref fails R2, description with 'secret' fails R3, unknown depends_on ID fails R4, valid task passes all rules

tasks.title: prd_generator_ralph_add
tasks.description: Implement PrdGenerator class and 'ralph add' CLI command. PrdGenerator accepts a natural language spec string or a GitHub issue URL. If a URL matching r'github\.com/.+/issues/\d+' is detected, fetches the issue body via 'gh issue view <number> --json title,body'. Calls AIRunner.run_reviewer() with PromptBuilder.prd_generate_prompt() to produce a JSON list of task dicts, validates each via PrdValidator, then appends to prd.json via TaskTracker.add_task(). Infers next epic prefix from highest existing task ID. See DESIGN.md: PrdGenerator.
acceptance_criteria: PrdGenerator(ai_runner: AIRunner, task_tracker: TaskTracker, validator: PrdValidator, runner: SubprocessRunner, logger: RalphLogger) constructor

generate(spec: str) -> list[dict]: detects GitHub issue URL, fetches body via gh CLI if URL, calls AIRunner with prd_generate_prompt, parses JSON response, validates each task via PrdValidator, appends to prd.json via TaskTracker.add_task()
PromptBuilder.prd_generate_prompt(spec: str, existing_tasks: list[dict]) -> str: includes existing task IDs and max epic prefix so AI generates correct IDs and depends_on chains
'ralph add' click command accepts a positional spec argument (string or URL), instantiates PrdGenerator, calls generate(), prints count of tasks added
Epic prefix inference: scans existing task IDs for highest Mx prefix, increments x for new tasks (e.g. if max is M1, new tasks get M2 prefix)
Tests mock AIRunner and SubprocessRunner; verify GitHub URL detection; verify PrdValidator called for each generated task; verify TaskTracker.add_task called N times

tasks.title: plan_consensus_ralph_plan
tasks.description: Implement PlanConsensus class and 'ralph plan' CLI command. PlanConsensus runs a Planner + Critic loop (max 3 iterations): Planner agent produces a work plan from a ProjectSpec or free-text brief; Critic agent reviews against quality gates (measurable ACs, atomic tasks, no vague language like 'improve' or 'enhance'); on REJECT the Critic feedback is sent back to the Planner; on OKAY or after 3 iterations the plan is written to ralph-plan.md and returned. See DESIGN.md: PlanConsensus.
acceptance_criteria: PlanConsensus(ai_runner: AIRunner, logger: RalphLogger, config: Config) constructor
run(brief: str, max_iterations: int = 3) -> str: Planner produces plan, Critic reviews, loops on REJECT up to max_iterations, returns final plan text
PromptBuilder.planner_prompt(brief: str, feedback: str = '') -> str: includes brief and any prior Critic feedback

PromptBuilder.critic_prompt(plan: str) -> str: instructs Critic to output OKAY or REJECT with specific line-level reasons; checks for vague verbs (improve, enhance, refactor, fix) without measurable outcomes
Critic verdict parsing: REJECT takes precedence if both strings appear; unclear verdict treated as OKAY with warning logged
After loop: writes plan to ralph-plan.md in repo root; logs iteration count and final verdict
'ralph plan' click command accepts optional --brief TEXT argument (or reads from stdin if omitted), calls PlanConsensus.run(), prints path to ralph-plan.md
Tests mock AIRunner; verify REJECT loops correctly; verify OKAY exits early; verify ralph-plan.md written; verify max_iterations respected

tasks.title: unit_tests_task_tracker_plan_checker
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for TaskTracker and PlanChecker classes in ralph.py. TaskTracker tests must verify: fresh load behavior (never cached state), get_next_task() filtering by completed=false, owner filtering excluding human tasks, depends_on validation ensuring only completed dependencies are eligible, mark_complete atomicity, and commit_tracking git command sequence. PlanChecker tests must verify: structural validation catching missing fields (title, description, acceptance_criteria), empty AC list detection, unresolved depends_on catching non-existent task IDs, complexity inference scoring based on AC count, word count, file count, and keyword matching. All tests must use tmp_path for filesystem isolation and mock SubprocessRunner to avoid actual git calls.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_task_tracker.py: test_fresh_load_reads_disk_each_call, test_get_next_task_returns_incomplete_ralph_owned_task, test_get_next_task_skips_human_owned_tasks, test_get_next_task_respects_depends_on_completion, test_mark_complete_writes_back_to_disk, test_commit_tracking_issues_correct_git_commands

tests/test_plan_checker.py: test_structural_validation_fails_missing_required_field, test_empty_acceptance_criteria_caught, test_unresolved_depends_on_raises_error, test_complexity_inference_scores_1_for_simple_tasks, test_complexity_inference_scores_2_for_moderate_tasks, test_complexity_inference_scores_3_for_complex_tasks
All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/ -v

tasks.title: unit_tests_branch_manager_prd_guard
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for BranchManager and PRDGuard classes in ralph.py. BranchManager tests must verify: SSH remote enforcement rejecting HTTPS remotes, ensure_main_up_to_date using reset --hard (never pull --ff-only), checkout_or_create raising BranchExistsError when resume=False and branch exists, sanitise_branch_name transforming titles to 40-char hyphenated lowercase, and that verify_ssh_remote() is called before every push operation. PRDGuard tests must verify: detecting any modification to prd.json via diff parsing, catching completed=true changes specifically, and raising PRDGuardViolation with the offending PR number and diff lines. All tests must mock SubprocessRunner and verify exact git command sequences via call inspection.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_branch_manager.py: test_verify_ssh_remote_raises_on_https_url, test_ensure_main_up_to_date_uses_reset_not_pull, test_checkout_or_create_raises_on_existing_branch_no_resume, test_checkout_or_create_creates_new_branch_when_not_exists, test_sanitise_branch_name_truncates_to_40_chars, test_push_verifies_ssh_before_executing
tests/test_prd_guard.py: test_empty_prd_diff_passes, test_any_prd_modification_raises_violation, test_completed_true_change_raises_violation, test_violation_includes_pr_number_and_offending_lines
All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/ -v

tasks.title: unit_tests_prompt_builder_ci_poller
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for PromptBuilder and CIPoller classes in ralph.py. PromptBuilder tests must verify: all methods are @staticmethod with no instance state, coder_prompt includes epic_addenda when task epic matches, reviewer_prompt includes all 6 review categories, test_writer_prompt instructs failing tests only, and decomposition prompt requests JSON list format. CIPoller tests must verify: _get_latest_run_id parses gh CLI JSON output correctly, _wait_for_run polls at CI_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS intervals, _wait_for_new_run detects when a new run ID appears, run-ID pinning prevents stale data race conditions, and conclusion field (not state) is used for pass/fail determination. All tests must mock SubprocessRunner and use controlled timing for poll loops.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_prompt_builder.py: test_all_methods_are_staticmethods, test_coder_prompt_includes_epic_addenda_when_matching, test_reviewer_prompt_includes_all_six_review_categories, test_test_writer_prompt_demands_failing_tests, test_decompose_prompt_requests_json_list

tests/test_ci_poller.py: test_get_latest_run_id_parses_gh_json_output, test_wait_for_run_polls_at_configured_interval, test_wait_for_new_run_detects_different_run_id, test_run_id_pinning_avoids_stale_results, test_uses_conclusion_field_not_state_for_verdict
All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/ -v

tasks.title: integration_tests_dry_run_smoke
tasks.description: Create integration test suite that performs a dry-run smoke test using a fixture prd.json. The test must invoke the full Orchestrator stack with --dry-run flag, verifying that all components initialize correctly, preflight checks pass, task selection identifies the correct next task, and no actual AI calls or git operations occur. The fixture prd.json must contain a representative mix of completed and pending tasks with various dependency chains. The test captures and asserts on dry-run output format, confirming task titles are printed and estimated actions are displayed. This test serves as the primary smoke test for release confidence, catching wiring issues and configuration drift.
acceptance_criteria: tests/integration/test_dry_run_smoke.py: creates fixture prd.json with 5 tasks (2 completed, 3 pending with dependencies), runs Orchestrator with --dry-run, verifies no git commands executed via SubprocessRunner mock, verifies no AI calls made via AIRunner mock, asserts output contains pending task titles, asserts output format matches expected dry-run template
tests/fixtures/dry_run_prd.json: representative prd.json fixture with mix of completed/pending tasks and dependency chains
Test runs and passes with uv run pytest tests/integration/test_dry_run_smoke.py -v

tasks.title: coverage_threshold_seventy_percent
tasks.description: Configure pytest-cov to enforce a minimum 70% line coverage threshold matching GSD's quality bar. This requires updating pyproject.toml to add pytest-cov dependency, configuring coverage to measure ralph.py specifically (excluding tests/ directory), and setting fail_under=70 in the coverage report configuration. Run the full test suite to establish baseline coverage, identify any gaps in existing tests, and add minimal tests to reach the threshold. Document the coverage command in quality_checks. The coverage report must generate both terminal output and HTML reports for debugging. This ensures the codebase maintains testability and prevents regression as new features are added.
acceptance_criteria: pyproject.toml: pytest-cov added to dev dependencies, coverage configuration with fail_under=70, source=ralph.py, omit=tests/*
quality_checks in prd.json updated to include uv run pytest tests/ --cov=ralph.py --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=70 -v
Coverage baseline report generated and stored, confirming ≥70% line coverage for ralph.py
HTML coverage report generated at htmlcov/index.html for debugging uncovered lines
All existing tests pass with new coverage configuration

tasks.title: validate_plan_flag_tier_two_ai
tasks.description: Implement the --validate-plan CLI flag that triggers PlanChecker Tier 2 AI sanity check using AIRunner. When --validate-plan is passed, the orchestrator must run an enhanced validation before starting the sprint. The AI validator receives all pending tasks via PromptBuilder.plan_check_prompt() and analyzes them for untestable acceptance criteria (vague language like 'works correctly' without measurable outcomes), non-atomic tasks (ACs that imply multiple distinct implementations), and unresolved contradictions between dependent tasks. Warnings are logged but do not block execution. The AI response is parsed for [WARN] task_id: reason lines. This catches planning errors early before ralph invests compute in implementation.
acceptance_criteria: ralph.py: --validate-plan flag added to CLI, mapped to Config.validate_plan: bool field
PlanChecker.run() accepts ai_check: bool parameter, when True calls AIRunner with plan_check_prompt
PromptBuilder.plan_check_prompt(tasks: list[dict]) -> str: instructs AI to flag untestable ACs, non-atomic tasks, contradictions using [WARN] task_id: reason format
Orchestrator._preflight() calls PlanChecker with ai_check=config.validate_plan when flag is set
Warnings are logged at WARN level but do not raise PlanInvalidError or block execution
tests/test_validate_plan.py: test_flag_triggers_ai_validation, test_warn_format_parsed_correctly, test_warnings_logged_but_do_not_block, test_no_ai_call_when_flag_false

tasks.title: loop_supervisor_clean_exit_verification
tasks.description: Implement LoopSupervisor class that monitors each sprint run for clean-exit verification. After every Orchestrator.run() completes (whether successful or via stop condition), the supervisor reads ralph.log and cross-checks for CLEAN_EXIT_MARKERS which include: explicit 'Sprint complete' log line, progress.txt update with timestamp, and no unhandled exception traces in the final 50 log lines. If any marker is missing, the supervisor logs a warning with specific missing indicators. The supervisor also maintains a run history in .ralph/run-history.json tracking: run timestamp, tasks completed count, final state (clean/unclean), and any fatal error classification. This provides observability for unattended operation confidence.
acceptance_criteria: ralph.py: LoopSupervisor(logger: RalphLogger, log_path: Path, progress_path: Path) class implemented
verify_clean_exit() -> CleanExitResult: reads ralph.log final 50 lines, checks for markers: 'Sprint complete', 'progress.txt updated', absence of 'Traceback' or 'Unhandled exception'
record_run(result: CleanExitResult, tasks_completed: int) appends entry to .ralph/run-history.json with fields: timestamp, tasks_completed, final_state (clean/unclean), fatal_error_type
Orchestrator.run() calls loop_supervisor.verify_clean_exit() and record_run() before returning
tests/test_loop_supervisor.py: test_detects_missing_sprint_complete_marker, test_detects_traceback_in_final_lines, test_records_run_history_correctly, test_clean_exit_all_markers_present

tasks.title: sprint_summary_report_markdown
tasks.description: Implement sprint summary report generation in markdown format output at the end of each Orchestrator.run(). The report must be written to ralph-summary-{timestamp}.md in the repo root and include sections: tasks completed (task IDs, titles, PR numbers), CI results per task (passed/failed with retry counts), PR merge status (merged/closed/pending), escalations (fatal errors with error types and task IDs), performance metrics (total runtime, iterations consumed), and a readiness score (percentage of tasks completed without escalation). The markdown must be parseable by the future Scrum Master layer (Milestone 4) using frontmatter YAML delimiters for structured data and a human-readable body. This provides post-sprint visibility for human review and automated tooling consumption.
acceptance_criteria: ralph.py: Orchestrator._generate_sprint_summary() -> str method creates markdown report
Report includes YAML frontmatter with: sprint_start, sprint_end, tasks_completed_count, total_tasks, fatal_errors_count, readiness_score_percent

Report body includes sections: Tasks Completed (table with ID, Title, PR, Status), CI Results (per-task pass/fail with rounds used), Escalations (error type, task ID, reason), Performance Metrics (runtime, iterations)
File written to ralph-summary-{timestamp}.md with ISO8601 timestamp in filename
Orchestrator.run() calls _generate_sprint_summary() before clean-exit verification

tests/test_sprint_summary.py: test_summary_file_created_with_correct_name, test_frontmatter_parseable_yaml, test_includes_all_completed_tasks, test_includes_ci_results_per_task, test_includes_escalation_section

tasks.title: progress_txt_human_readable_format
tasks.description: Refactor TaskTracker.append_progress() to write progress.txt in a human-readable format optimized for quick post-sprint review. The new format must use clear visual separators (ASCII table or markdown), include relative timestamps (e.g., '2 hours ago'), group tasks by epic, and show status indicators (✓ for completed, ⚠ for escalated, ⏸ for pending). Add a header row with sprint metadata (start date, current iteration count). The format must remain machine-parseable for future automation while prioritizing human readability. Update the progress.txt template in DESIGN.md if it differs. This ensures the human operator can quickly assess sprint health without parsing JSON or dense log files.
acceptance_criteria: ralph.py: TaskTracker.append_progress() updated to write markdown-table format with headers: Epic | Task ID | Title | Status | Completed | PR
Status column uses symbols: ✓ for completed without escalation, ⚠ for completed with escalation/retry exhaustion, ⏸ for in-progress/pending
Relative timestamps added in Completed column (e.g., '2024-01-15 14:30 (2h ago)')
Header section includes sprint start date and iteration count
Visual separator line after every 5 tasks for readability
tests/test_task_tracker.py updated: test_append_progress_writes_human_readable_format, test_symbols_correct_for_completed_tasks, test_header_includes_sprint_metadata
Sample progress.txt generated and verified for readability
